channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Johnson & Johnson Foundation Rolls Out Sh500,000 Fund to Support Kenyan Nurses' Education
item.description: The Johnson & Johnson Foundation has officially rolled out its Advanced Nursing Education Fund in Kenya, offering study loans of up to Sh500,000 to nurses and midwives who want to advance their educat...

item.title: Escaped Sablayan Prison inmate recaptured after motorcycle theft alert
item.description: Muling nahuli ng mga awtoridad ang isang bilanggo na tumakas mula sa Sablayan Prison and Penal Farm sa Occidental Mindoro matapos makatanggap ng ulat ang pulisya tungkol sa ninakaw na motorsiklo. Kini...

item.title: Wayne Bennett returns to South Sydney with new signings, while Ricky Stuart faces big challenges with Canberra
item.description: Wayne Bennett has experienced a mixed few years, with his aura taking a hit when he couldn’t secure a superstar for the Dolphins, but regained some shine after signing David Fifita and Payne Haas for ...

item.title: Housing, Safety and Livelihood: Perspectives of Women Heading Households in South Africa
item.description: South Africa continues to face a significant housing deficit, with an estimated 2 million units still required despite government delivery of over 5 million houses and subsidies since 1994. Women head...

item.title: Iran Issues Strong Warning Following U.S. Military Strike on Warship
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East escalated sharply after Iran issued a strong warning following the U.S. sinking of an Iranian warship. Iranian officials called for retaliation against former U.S. Presiden...

item.title: Victims and Lawyers Face Challenges Attending Duterte's ICC Confirmation of Charges Hearing
item.description: This in-depth article from Rappler recounts the difficult experiences of drug war victims, their families, lawyers, and advocates as they journey to The Hague for the confirmation of charges hearing a...

item.title: Government Clarifies No Ports Are Temporarily Closed in Pakistan
item.description: The Government of Pakistan on Thursday clarified that a notification circulating on social media, claiming a temporary suspension of all port entries until March 10, is completely false. The Informati...

item.title: Quezon City Congressman Faces Criticism for Lewd Remarks Against Actress Anne Curtis
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Recently, Quezon City 4th District Representative Bong Suntay made lewd remarks about actress Anne Curtis, sparking public outrage. Many netizens expressed concern that such comm...

item.title: Daily Superquiz Puzzle for Friday, 6 March 2026
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald presents its daily Superquiz for Friday, 6 March 2026. Readers are invited to test their general knowledge and puzzle-solving skills through this interactive feature. Alongsi...

item.title: Three Indian Nationals Killed in Attacks on Merchant Vessels Near Oman
item.description: Three Indian nationals lost their lives in attacks on two merchant vessels off the coast of Oman. Among the deceased was 25-year-old Dixit Solanki from Mumbai, who was aboard the MKD Vyom, a vessel fl...

item.title: CAF reschedules 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations to July-August
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) don officially announce say the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) don shift to new dates. The tournament wey suppose hold for Morocco from 17 Marc...

item.title: How Financial Literacy Can Help Women Overcome Wealth Gaps
item.description: The article explores why women face significant challenges in building wealth and how financial literacy can help bridge the gap. Wealth, which includes savings, property, and retirement plans, is a k...

item.title: Pakistan voices non-proliferation concerns over India–Canada long-term uranium supply agreement
item.description: Pakistan’s Foreign Office has expressed serious concern over a long-term uranium supply agreement recently concluded between India and Canada, calling it “yet another country-specific exception” in th...

item.title: Labour's Mahmood announces major reforms to tighten UK's asylum system
item.description: Shabana Mahmood, the Home Secretary, has acknowledged that migration is a challenging issue for the Labour Party as she unveiled significant reforms to the UK's asylum system. Mahmood emphasised that ...

item.title: CAF Reschedules WAFCON 2026 to July Amid Unforeseen Issues
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) has announced that the Women's Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON), originally set to kick off on March 17, 2026, in Morocco, has been postponed. The tournament ...

item.title: Trump Administration and Tech Giants Sign Nonbinding Pledge on Data Center Energy Costs Amid Skepticism
item.description: On March 4, 2026, President Donald Trump hosted representatives from major tech companies including Microsoft, Meta, OpenAI, xAI, Google/Alphabet, Oracle, and Amazon at the White House to sign a volun...

item.title: AU Leaders Discuss Continental Unity and Economic Independence Amid Global Unrest
item.description: During the recent African Union summit in Addis Ababa, leaders discussed ways to strengthen Africa's unity and independence in face of global instability. With tensions rising among Western countries ...

item.title: Plateau PDP Sets Up Caretaker Committee in Shendam LGA and Appoints New Chairman for Wase LGA
item.description: The Plateau State chapter of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) has taken decisive steps to reorganise its structure in two local government areas. Dem say dem appoint a nine-member caretaker committe...

item.title: Three Entrepreneurs with Disabilities Receive 17m/- for Winning Wazo Bora Pitch Competition
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, three entrepreneurs with disabilities have been awarded a total of 17 million Tanzanian shillings as seed capital after winning the Wazo Bora Pitch Competition. The contest featured ...

item.title: Manipuri Film 'Boong' Wins BAFTA, Paul Thomas Anderson’s 'One Battle After Another' Dominates Awards
item.description: Indian filmmaker Lakshmipriya Devi has made a remarkable entry into international cinema with her debut feature 'Boong', winning the Best Children’s & Family Film award at the 2026 BAFTAs. The Manipur...
